issue_title: Growth, Water Scarcity, and Long-Term Supply in Nevada

dataset_scope.sample_size_note: 88 bills across four sessions — sample is large enough for pattern detection within the keyword-discovered set; patterns are not claimed for an exhaustive universe of all Nevada water bills.

bipartisan_sponsorship.note: Individual sponsorship facts mark these three 2019 bills; aggregate F-478 counts four multi-party bills across the full 88-bill set.
No 2021 bill appears among the multi-party sponsorship signals captured in synthesis sponsorship facts.
No 2023 bill appears among the multi-party sponsorship signals captured in synthesis sponsorship facts.

F-478 aggregate counts four multi-party bills in the dataset. Processed sponsor party labels confirm SB 276 (2025) as the fourth; no separate 2025 sponsorship-fact extract with the multi-party signal was present in synthesis beyond the aggregate.

topic_patterns.label: Groundwater basin and groundwater management legislation
topic_patterns.outcome_detail: Several 2019 groundwater/basin bills enacted (SB 140, AB 95, SB 236); 2023 groundwater management plan bill SB 113 enacted; 2023 groundwater basin assessment (SB 112) and groundwater conservation (SB 176) bills failed before/at origin chamber.

topic_patterns.label: Groundwater boards revisions
topic_patterns.outcome_detail: All three groundwater-board bills in the set failed: SB 149 stalled in origin committee; SB 180 reached bicameral milestones but did not become signed law; AB 363 did not pass origin chamber.

topic_patterns.label: Water conservation plan and conservation statutory revisions
topic_patterns.outcome_detail: AB 163 (2019), AB 356 (2021), AB 191 (2023), and AB 220 (2023) enacted; AB 134 (2025) failed in origin committee.

topic_patterns.label: Division of Water Resources and water-infrastructure appropriations
topic_patterns.outcome_detail: SB 509, AB 465, SB 472, and SB 473 enacted as appropriations to DWR or related water infrastructure/study uses; SB 102 (grants for water resource plans) failed in origin committee.

topic_patterns.label: Colorado River Commission and Colorado River protection measures
topic_patterns.outcome_detail: SB 76 and AB 15 failed in origin committee; SJR 3 (Colorado River protection urging) recorded as In Progress after enrollment/delivery to Secretary of State.

topic_patterns.label: State Engineer / NRS 533 appropriation and adjudication framework
topic_patterns.outcome_detail: NRS 533 cited in digests/titles of 26 bills; State Engineer referenced for 41 bills. Outcomes mix enactment (e.g., SB 140, SB 236, AB 6) and failure (e.g., AB 30, AB 51, AB 5, SB 31).

topic_patterns.label: Community and public water system measures
topic_patterns.outcome_detail: SB 216 (community water systems), SB 238 (public water systems), and AB 186 (drinking water at food establishments) all failed without leaving origin committee under Joint Standing Rule 14.3.1 signals.

topic_patterns.label: Water banks authorization
topic_patterns.outcome_detail: Single observed water-banks authorization bill failed in origin committee.
topic_patterns.certainty_note: INSUFFICIENT FOR STRONG CONCLUSION — only one supporting bill instance in the keyword-discovered set.

topic_patterns.label: Drought planning / water resources advisory board creation
topic_patterns.outcome_detail: SB 499 to create Advisory Board on Water Resources Planning and Drought Resiliency failed without leaving origin committee.
topic_patterns.certainty_note: INSUFFICIENT FOR STRONG CONCLUSION — only one supporting bill instance.

entries.impact_note: IMPACT_DATA_NOT_AVAILABLE for 2019 enacted bills in this set — no agency reports, fiscal evaluation documents, or interim LCB evaluation memos were present in synthesis.
IMPACT_DATA_NOT_AVAILABLE for 2021 enacted bills in this set — no agency/fiscal evaluation documents linked in synthesis.
IMPACT_DATA_NOT_AVAILABLE for 2023 enacted bills in this set (enactment more than two years before analysis date) — no agency/fiscal evaluation documents linked in synthesis.
2025 enactments are within two years of the analysis date; 20-year impact evaluation was not applied to these bills under Analyzer rules.

stall_points.summary: Among 40 failed bills in the keyword-discovered set, the dominant stall point is origin committee (26 bills). Secondary stalls: origin chamber without passage (7), second-chamber committee (5), and bicameral passage without signed law (2). No failed bills in this set are recorded as executive vetoes.
stall_points.inference: This is an inference from the failed_or_stalled milestone signals in F-051–F-463 (failed bucket), not a primary source statement that committees are the institutional cause of non-passage.

identified_bills.note: Counted in F-478 aggregate; individual multi-party sponsorship extract not present among the 40 sponsorship facts in synthesis; confirmed against processed sponsor party labels solely to resolve which bill completes the aggregate count of 4.

bipartisan_signals.statement: Bipartisan sponsorship appeared on 4 of 88 bills.

viability_indicators.text: Historically, bills on groundwater boards have failed without enactment across the 2021, 2023, and 2025 sessions in this dataset.
Historically, bills on water conservation statutory revisions have often enacted in 2019–2023, with at least one 2025 conservation bill failing in origin committee.

viability_indicators.allowed_phrase_form: Enacted legislation on [topic] is [rare / occasional / common] in the last 4 sessions.
viability_indicators.text: Enacted legislation on water-related subjects in this keyword-discovered set is common in the last 4 sessions (45 of 88 bills enacted).

viability_indicators.allowed_phrase_form: Enacted legislation on [topic] is [rare / occasional / common] in the last 4 sessions.
viability_indicators.text: Enacted legislation on Division of Water Resources appropriations is common in the last 4 sessions within this dataset (multiple enacted appropriations in 2019, 2021, and 2023).

viability_indicators.allowed_phrase_form: Bipartisan sponsorship appeared on [N] of [M] bills.
viability_indicators.text: Bipartisan sponsorship appeared on 4 of 88 bills.

viability_indicators.allowed_phrase_form: Data gaps prevent assessment of [specific subtopic].
viability_indicators.text: Data gaps prevent assessment of post-enactment policy impact for laws in the 20-year lookback window.

viability_indicators.allowed_phrase_form: Data gaps prevent assessment of [specific subtopic].
viability_indicators.text: Data gaps prevent assessment of exhaustive bill-universe coverage beyond this keyword-discovered set.
Historically, bills on community and public water systems in this set have stalled in origin committee.

data_gaps.description: Keyword-discovered dataset is not an exhaustive universe of all Nevada water bills.
OpenStates detail enrichment largely unavailable; NELIS was primary bill-detail source.
No interim LCB memos or agency evaluation reports in sources for impact analysis.
No Forum Phase 1 or Phase 2 process materials available; forum_process_input empty.
Vote records include missing yes/no counts on some rows.
Sponsorship party labels incomplete for many committee-sponsored bills; multi-party signal available for only a subset.
